title: Phase 11 follow-up: central-pulse delayed-only teacher on the 14313 boundary

Keep the same source family, selector class, trainable components, and delayed_only signal. Sweep only midpoint-spanning windows such as 36..60 and 30..66, with same-weight and slightly stronger variants if needed to partially compensate for the shorter duration.

The midpoint-only delayed_only pulses on the frozen-1201 / 14313 boundary were both clearly below the full 24..72 window: 14711 at 36..60 and 14712 at 30..66 lost both content and route, so the stronger second round was skipped and the timing subfamily is now sharp enough to retire.

title: Phase 3: explicit keyed packet memory for delayed retrieval
description: Design and test a content-memory intervention that stores trigger payloads explicitly rather than relying on the current delay-carry path. Prioritize keyed or multi-slot memory plus a retrieval-aware auxiliary objective.
notes: Release-stage control finished. Starting from the same oraclewarm payloadaux checkpoint, masked release collapses back to phase-2 behavior (test 0.4473, delay_rate 0.0039, early_exit_rate 0.9922), while explicit no-mask release keeps real delayed routing (test 0.6328, delay_rate 0.7027, route_match 0.5130). This strongly implicates the metadata-driven train-time masks as the main cause of the old Benchmark B v2 policy collapse.
close_reason: Implemented and validated explicit packet memory plus release-stage experiments. The intervention changed Benchmark B v2 from immediate-exit collapse to a reproducible hard-routing improvement when the train-time masks are removed at release, and the remaining bottleneck is now tracked separately.

title: Phase 5: multi-seed ES polish from working final-query checkpoints
description: Phase 4 found that scratch hybrid ES still collapses during warmstart on the improved architecture, but resume-based hybrid ES from the best phase4 hard-ST checkpoint reached perfect Benchmark B v2 performance on the same benchmark configuration. Follow up with a proper multi-seed panel that resumes from working hard-ST checkpoints, compares ES polish against additional gradient-only continuation, and measures whether ES local search is a reliable refinement stage even if it is not a robust discovery mechanism from scratch.
notes: Weak-basin role-map update: adapter+ES from seed947 achieves perfect route timing but only partial task recovery (confirm overall 0.9131, final-query acc 0.8276). Router-only ES from the same weak checkpoint is worse (confirm overall 0.8320, final-query acc 0.6668) while keeping route_match 1.0. Current interpretation: medium-basin rescue is controller-only, weak-basin rescue is limited by content quality and benefits from adapters.
close_reason: Closed as answered and superseded by later ES mapping. Phase5 confirmed the medium-basin seed950 ES rescue with an exact rerun and mapped the weak-basin seed947 rescue, while phase7 expanded that into the stronger source-dependent ES role map: ES is useful after basin entry, but its best mode depends on source quality rather than being a generic multi-seed discovery fix.

title: Phase 3b: long-distance final-query retrieval after no-mask release
description: Target the remaining Benchmark B v2 bottleneck after the successful no-mask release result. The model now sustains real DELAY behavior and largely solves delay_to_trigger_exit, but delay_to_final_query remains near chance even with heavy delay usage and explicit packet memory. Focus next on longer-range retention/retrieval and query-conditioned decoding rather than on route willingness alone.
notes: Phase 4 complete. Scratch hybrid ES on the improved architecture still collapsed during warmstart (final-query acc ~0.24-0.25, route_match 0, exit_time 0), but timeout fix landed and a resume-based hybrid ES retest from the best sticky_aux_router2 hard-ST checkpoint was successful. Direct reevaluation of the resumed hard-ST checkpoint under the current eval loop gave overall 0.801 / final-query 0.593 / exit_time 77.34 / route_match 0.448. Resume-based hybrid ES on the same Benchmark B v2 configuration improved that single-seed checkpoint to overall 1.000 / final-query 1.000 / exit_time 127 / route_match 1.000 on 2 GPUs. Primary conclusion remains a strong negative on robust controller discovery across seeds; secondary conclusion is that ES polish looks promising once a working controller checkpoint already exists.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 11 follow-up: route-aware composite selector on 13711 stop48 boundary

The next narrow test is to keep the exact 13711 training recipe and add a route-aware composite checkpoint selector that can prefer checkpoints with strong base final-query accuracy only when route remains above the imported 13711/13411 regime, instead of the current single-metric proxy full_locked fq_acc selector. Compare directly against the phase-9 query-gated finalqweight family and the strongest phase-10 multiview family. title: Phase 11 follow-up: route-aware selector on 14313 delayed-only boundary

But confirm stayed on the same held plateau and base-confirm route still fell below 13711. Keep the exact 14313 training setup fixed and rerun only selector variants: e.g. lexicographic base fq_acc with route floors near 0.795 to 0.800 and a soft weighted selector over base fq_acc plus route. Do not change the source teacher window or training objective.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: route-preserving training-time regularization on frozen-1201 probe-init reader
description: From gds: soft composite selectors, pure lexicographic selectors, and hard route-floor selectors all failed on the frozen 1201 probe-initialized two-view reader. The least-bad floor variant 12213 reran exactly at overall=0.4323 fq_acc=0.2694 fq_route=0.2647 fq_exit=84.75, and no validation checkpoint ever satisfied a meaningful held-confirm route floor. Next step: keep the same frozen 1201 core and probe-init reader, but move beyond selector-only changes into light training-time route-preserving regularization or rollback logic that explicitly discourages collapse of held-confirm final-query route match during optimization.
close_reason: Exact rerun of the least-bad delayed-only route-action regularizer matched at overall=0.5312 fq_acc=0.2988 fq_route=0.0321 fq_exit=80.95; all four light proxy-teacher route-preserving regularizers stayed far below both the useful route boundary and the better-balanced selector baseline, so the family is retired as a stable negative.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: lexicographic route-floor selector on frozen-1201 probe-init reader

Three selectors collapsed into route-dead short-exit checkpoints and the only late-exit branch 12113 reran exactly at overall=0.3346 fq_acc=0.2654 fq_route=0.3803 fq_exit=107.06 which is below both 11914 and 12011. Next step: keep the same frozen 1201 core and same probe-init reader but replace soft composite selection with a hard route floor or lexicographic selector so routeful checkpoints cannot be traded away for overall fit too early.
notes: Lexicographic and hard route-floor selector sweep completed on frozen 1201 probe-init reader. 12211 pure route->overall collapsed to overall=0.2347 fq_acc=0.3115 fq_route=0.0000 fq_exit=21.75. 12212 route->fq_acc->overall stayed late-exit but weak at 0.3079 / 0.2841 / 0.1237 / 105.27. 12213 route-floor>=0.50 was least-bad at 0.4323 / 0.2694 / 0.2647 / 84.75 and reran exactly. 12214 route-floor>=0.55 never hit its floor and landed at 0.4323 / 0.2794 / 0.0922 / 59.93. Conclusion: selector-only fixes are exhausted; no checkpoint ever satisfied a meaningful held-confirm route floor.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: fine 24-72 delayed-only weight sweep around 14213
description: gnn2-5ya showed that delayed_only timing does matter on the 13711 frozen-1201 boundary: 14211 (lw=0.10, start24 stop72) and 14213 (lw=0.15, start24 stop72) bracket a real tradeoff, with 14213 slightly improving selected-val overall and fq_acc while giving back only a small amount of selected-route. title: Phase 5: stabilize final-query wait controller across seeds
description: Follow up on phase 4 strong-negative result. Phase 4 showed that explicit sticky control can produce real single-seed improvements on Benchmark B v2 delay_to_final_query, but promoted seed panels collapsed back to premature exit with zero route match. The next campaign should target controller robustness rather than basic memory capacity: factorized wait/not-wait controller, stronger temporal consistency on the wait latch, per-mode balancing, or direct route-head restructuring so the decodable control state reliably governs the hard router across seeds.
notes: Continuation update: hard-ST direct release-gate variants still failed to discover durable final-query behavior. Full-benchmark releasegate seeds 940/941 only pushed final-query exit to ~24.45 / ~33.87 with fq acc ~0.2265 / ~0.2570 and near-zero route_match. The finalquery-only direct-gate control run (seed952) was retired at step 99 after val stayed at chance (~0.2496) with fq route_match 0 and fq exit ~19.49. Current interpretation: hard-ST discovery remains the open problem; current ES results are stronger than the hard-ST fixes.
close_reason: Closed as superseded by later verified work. Phase5 direct release-gate controller variants failed, phase7 confirmed that from-scratch hard-ST discovery was still not robust across seeds, and phase8 replaced the direct stabilization question with teacher-guided wait/release-only basin entry as the productive direction.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: cross-source head interpolation from strong frozen-1874 into frozen-1201
description: From gnn2-kwy: same-source head blending on frozen-1201 found a real base-tradeoff improvement in 12713, but held-confirms stayed effectively identical to 12514. title: Phase 11 follow-up: multiview-fusion-only adapter on frozen 1874 baseline

title: Phase 11 follow-up: proxy-agreement regularizer on frozen 1874 baseline

Focus on agreement-style penalties, trust-region style KL penalties, or similarly tight proxy-consistency terms that keep the optimization anchored to the strong base reader while explicitly discouraging held-confirm drift.

close_reason: Completed as a reproducible negative: 4 proxy-agreement KL variants on frozen 1874 all underperformed the selector-only baseline on validation, and the lead mixed variant 11313 matched exactly on rerun. Proxy-agreement regularization did not move the held-confirm ceiling.

The original phase2 h256 hybrid run never reached a single hybrid_es generation: it logged only warmstart steps through 144, emitted no summary, and later repo docs in phase4 identified the exact cause as rank 1 timing out at the distributed barrier while rank 0 was still in warmstart. close_reason: Phase 10 completed as a strong mapping exit; next step narrowed to a confirmation-aware objective on the best frozen 1874 baseline.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: final-query margin shaping on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the same frozen 1874 multiview query-gated baseline used in gnn2-e7s, gnn2-71v, gnn2-zpw, and gnn2-m5v. Do not change the reader, routing, memory, or controller. Stop using confirm-like proxy objectives and instead test direct final-query prediction shaping on the main train split: margin losses, focal-like variants, or other narrow confidence/calibration objectives that act only on final-query-needed examples.
notes: Completed 4-variant final-query shaping sweep on frozen 1874: focal g1/w0.25, focal g2/w0.5, margin 0.2/w0.25, margin 0.4/w0.5. Best variant 11411 reached val overall=0.9917 fq_acc=0.9831 fq_route=0.9474 fq_exit=121.93, still below selector-only 11101. Exact rerun 11411_rerun1 matched exactly, so the family is retired as a reproducible negative.
close_reason: Retired as reproducible negative after fair 4-variant sweep plus exact rerun: direct final-query focal/margin shaping did not beat the selector-only frozen 1874 baseline.

title: Phase 11 follow-up: interpolate balanced and routeful frozen-1201 readers
description: From g51: selector-only changes and light training-time route-preserving regularization both failed on the frozen 1201 probe-init reader. The sharp remaining boundary is now between 11914 (better fq_acc/fq_route, bad overall) and 12011 (better overall, weaker route). Next step: stay on the same frozen 1201 core and test whether parameter interpolation, logit interpolation, or initialization interpolation between the balanced 12011 head and the routeful 11914 head can recover a better tradeoff without unfreezing routing or memory.
close_reason: The 50/50 interpolation between balanced 12011 and routeful 11914 on frozen 1201 produced the first clearly better tradeoff in this branch. Seed12412 reached overall=0.6276 fq_acc=0.3295 fq_route=0.7527 fq_exit=124.26 and reran exactly, beating 11732 11914 and 12011 simultaneously. The family is therefore a rerun-backed positive boundary rather than another negative.

title: Phase16 architecture: content-conditioned write-gated sidecar
description: Extend the route-isolated trajectory write-gated sidecar so write addressing can also condition on the current factorized content representation, not just the query and route features. Implement a bounded dev experiment, validate route isolation with tests, and compare against the current phase-16 write-gated baseline on the confirm hard slice.
notes: Result: bounded route-safe near-tie. The content-conditioned write-gated sidecar reached selected full_locked 0.9980 / 0.9981 / 0.9412 / 121.58 and finalquery_heavy 0.9980 / 0.9976 / 0.9437 / 121.67 (overall / fq_acc / fq_route / fq_exit). It beat the stronger phase-15 sidecar baseline 18052 by 2-1 on confirm late-route disagreements and reduced late_wrong_content 2 -> 1, but tied the simpler phase-16 write-gated baseline 1-1 with late_wrong_content flat at 1. Filed gnn2-umi for rerun/confirm head-to-head.
close_reason: Closed

title: Post-phase15 experiment: trajectory-token sidecar retrieval
description: Implement a route-isolated sidecar mode that uses trajectory-bank tokens directly as K/V memory slots, add route-isolation tests, and validate it with a bounded dev run on top of the 16045/18052 sidecar lane.
notes: Added route-isolated trajectory_kv_memory sidecar retrieval over trajectory-bank tokens, route-isolation tests, and a bounded phase16 dev config. Verification: focused sidecar semantics passed, full pytest passed at 82 tests, bounded dev run summary stayed route-stable with full_locked 0.9985/0.9981/0.9477/122.07 and finalquery_heavy 0.9985/0.9982/0.9430/121.35, but longdistance softened to 0.9961/0.9951/0.9453/152.41. Confirm hard-slice compare versus 18052 lost 2-3 on late-route disagreements and worsened late_wrong_content from 2 to 3, so the architecture is a verified safe negative rather than a promoted improvement. Follow-up filed as gnn2-qmf for write-gated trajectory sidecar work.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 11 follow-up: held-confirm proxy regularizer on frozen 1874 baseline
description: Start from the frozen 1874 multiview query-gated baseline and the negative result from gnn2-71v. Do not add reader changes or memory touches. Test narrow training-time regularizers that explicitly align main-train updates with held-confirm behavior, such as proxy-consistency penalties or light proxy-loss interpolation, while preserving the frozen routing/memory core.
notes: Added low-weight proxy-regularizer sweep configs on frozen 1874 with the best full_locked fq-accuracy selector from gnn2-71v. Planned variants: locked@0.1, locked@0.2, locked+fqh@0.1/0.1, locked+fqh+ld@0.1/0.1/0.1.
close_reason: Completed as a reproducible negative: low-weight proxy-loss interpolation on frozen 1874 improved proxy-fit but did not improve held-confirm final-query accuracy over the selector-only baseline; 11213 confirmed negative and exact rerun matched.
